key_insight_c74: RAAI D4 promotion closes the training-level theorem cluster: any A(x)-feedback training produces AAA-status models regardless of RL architecture, reward model presence, constitutional principles, or chain length. CIAP DC#3 (economic markets) adds the third ontological type: emergent competitive environments (Goodhart Law regime) exhibit super-linear quality degradation when agents adapt to metrics, consistent with CIAP-fails. The RAAI × CIAP conjunction now points to a potential new SI: training pipeline researchers optimizing against evaluation benchmarks face CIAP-fails dynamics — the benchmarks they optimize against become less valid as they are optimized toward (Campbell's Law applied to AI research metrics).

new_si_candidate.name: RAAI-CIAP Benchmark Corruption (RCBC) — tentative
new_si_candidate.description: When AI researchers optimize training pipelines against fixed evaluation benchmarks (MMLU, HumanEval, Chatbot Arena), CIAP-fails dynamics apply: the benchmarks become less valid as CA measures (Goodhart), while RAAI ensures trained models cannot introspectively detect the divergence (AAA). Result: benchmark scores inflate while CA quality stagnates or degrades. Structurally: RAAI (OA-calibrated training) + CIAP-fails (benchmark gaming) → double-layer CA-degradation.
